title: The Trade-Through Rule and Locked and Crossed Markets Provisions of Regulation NMS
raw_text:
The Trade-Through Rule and Locked and Crossed Markets Provisions of Regulation NMS

The Securities and Exchange Commission ("Commission" or "SEC") is proposing amendments to Regulation NMS ("Regulation NMS") under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 ("Exchange Act"). The proposed amendments would rescind the trade-through rule for NMS stocks, the provision regarding locking and crossing quotations for NMS stocks, and certain defined terms. The proposed amendments would also make conforming changes to other related provisions.

title: MIAX Options Exchange - Symbol to Cloud Allocation Rebalance
raw_text:
MIAX Options — Regulatory Alert, Technical Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Options Exchange - Symbol to Cloud Allocation Rebalance
Regulatory Alert, Technical Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Options June 18, 2026 | 01:02 PM MIAX Options Exchange - Symbol to Cloud Allocation Rebalance The MIAX Options Exchange will rebalance symbol to cloud allocations effective Monday, July 20, 2026. Application layer connectivity testing will be available for firms to confirm changes on Saturday, July 18, 2026 between 8:30 a.m. and 10:30 a.m. ET. Symbol Range to Cloud Allocation: A (with the exception of symbols AAPL, AMD and AMZN) B C D-F G-H (with the exception of symbols GOOG and GOOGL) I J-L M (with the exception of symbols META and MU) N O P-R (with the exception of symbol QQQ) S (with the exception of symbols SNDK, SPCX and SPY) T-U (with the exception of symbol TSLA) V-Y Z AAPL, AMZN AMD GOOG, GOOGL META, SNDK MU QQQ TSLA SPCX SPY Market Data Dissemination: Market Data Feed content for each symbol will be disseminated across the assigned Cloud multicast addresses for: Top of Market (ToM) Feed Complex Top of Market (cToM) Feed MIAX Order Feed (MOR) Administrative Information Subscriber (AIS) Feed MIAX Express Interface (MEI) customers will need to direct their interface activity for options to their MEI sessions for each Cloud FIX Order Interface (FOI), Clearing Trade Drop (CTD) and FIX Drop Copy (FXD) customers are not impacted For additional details, please visit MIAX Options Interface Specifications . If you have any questions or to arrange testing, please contact Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302.

title: MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Reminder: Upcoming Enhancements - Live on June 22 and 25
raw_text:
MIAX Sapphire — Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert, Technical Alert — MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Reminder: Upcoming Enhancements - Live on June 22 and 25
Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert, Technical Alert MIAX Sapphire June 17, 2026 | 01:55 PM MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Reminder: Upcoming Enhancements - Live on June 22 and 25 As previously announced in the May 21, 2026 and June 4, 2026 Alerts, the following functionality for the MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange will be available in production on June 22 and 25, 2026. Increased Options Leg Ratio - Available Monday, June 22, 2026 This feature supports the increase of the Options leg ratio for Complex orders up to 999,999 Consistent with current handling, The Order Quantity multiplied by the highest Option Leg Ratio of the strategy must be less than or equal to 999,999 The Order Quantity multiplied by the Stock Leg Ratio of the strategy, if present, must be less than or equal to 99,999,999 This enhancement is contained within the FOI Interface (2.3a) release Certification is not required to use this feature in production Selective Liquidity Auto Purge (SLAP) Functionality - Available Thursday, June 25, 2026 This optional feature enhances the Mass Cancellation functionality by providing Members with the ability to remove a subset of its orders in the System With the introduction of a new “SLAP Codes” field in the Standard Binary Order – New Message, Members can specify custom SLAP codes, numbered 1 through 8, on some or all of its liquidity, providing more granular control of the Liquidity Mass Cancel Request when attempting to “Selectively Purge” specific liquidity The SLAP enhancement is contained within the MEO Interface (v1.2) release This feature requires certification prior to being used in production Additional Details These enhancements are currently available in the MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange's Firm Test Bed (FTB2) environment Updated specifications can be found at MIAX Sapphire Options Interface Specifications Please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302 to update configurations, arrange interface testing and schedule certification.

raw_text:
MIAX Options — Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Change to the Market for Underlying Security Used for Openings on MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options, and MIAX Sapphire Options for Symbol FITB Effective Friday, June 12, 2026
Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Options June 11, 2026 | 12:00 PM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Change to the Market for Underlying Security Used for Openings on MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options, and MIAX Sapphire Options for Symbol FITB Effective Friday, June 12, 2026 Fifth Third Bancorp (“FITB”) will be transferring primary listing from NASDAQ to the New York Stock Exchange (“NYSE”) effective tomorrow, Friday, June 12, 2026. MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options, and MIAX Sapphire Options will use NYSE as the “market for the underlying security” for the purpose of Openings on the Exchange. title: MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Reminder: Planned network switch expansion for the 10G ULL extranet and mandatory testing
raw_text:
MIAX Sapphire — Technical Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Reminder: Planned network switch expansion for the 10G ULL extranet and mandatory testing
Technical Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Sapphire June 10, 2026 | 10:09 AM MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Reminder: Planned network switch expansion for the 10G ULL extranet and mandatory testing As previously announced in the May 14, 2026 Alert , the MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange will expand the number of customer facing 10G ULL extranet edge switches from six to ten switches on Friday, July 17, 2026 post close of trading. For the four additional extranet edge switches, if firms would like to order new Cross Connects (CC) to coincide with the activation of the four additional extranet edge switches, Letters of Authorization (LOAs) are required and must be requested by June 18, 2026. Following the upgrade, firms must conduct mandatory testing for all new CCs. Also, testing is recommended for existing CC customers. Details on testing: Testing will be held on Saturday, July 18, 2026 from 9:00 a.m. to 12:00 p.m. EDT. Firms are required to perform the following verifications: Verify all 10G ULL CCs by logging into each subscribed application for each CC (i.e. establish application-level socket connections to MEO, MEI, FOI, CTD, etc., for all ports, across all CCs) and verify the login. Confirm heartbeat receipt for both the A & B market data feeds for all subscribed multicast feeds across each CC. After verifying connectivity, firms must inform MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com . Please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302 to arrange testing or to receive additional information on the planned network refresh.

title: MIAX Pearl Options Exchange - LC Cross Connect Availability for the 10G ULL Extranet
raw_text:
MIAX Pearl — Technical Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Pearl Options Exchange - LC Cross Connect Availability for the 10G ULL Extranet
Technical Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Pearl June 10, 2026 | 09:49 AM MIAX Pearl Options Exchange - LC Cross Connect Availability for the 10G ULL Extranet MIAX Pearl Options is now offering LC Cross Connects (CC) on its 10G ULL Extranet. Existing SC CCs will continue to be supported. Firms wishing to convert from SC to LC CCs need to obtain new LOAs and schedule cutovers with MIAX Trading Operations. All CC conversions must be one-for-one replacements of existing CCs without logical or switch port allocation level changes for conversions. New LC CCs will be tested using Optical Time-Domain Reflectometers (OTDR) in order to comply with existing MIAX Pearl Options Exchange standards. Please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302 to request an LOA, arrange testing or to receive additional information on the planned network refresh.

title: MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Update: SpaceX Cloud Allocation Multicast Addresses and Symbol Range Changes on MIAX Sapphire Clouds 6 and 7
raw_text:
MIAX Pearl — Technical Alert, Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Update: SpaceX Cloud Allocation Multicast Addresses and Symbol Range Changes on MIAX Sapphire Clouds 6 and 7
Technical Alert, Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert MIAX Pearl June 09, 2026 | 10:20 AM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Update: SpaceX Cloud Allocation Multicast Addresses and Symbol Range Changes on MIAX Sapphire Clouds 6 and 7 As previously announced in the June 5, 2026 Alert , the MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options, and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchanges will list options on SpaceX (symbol SPCX) once they are approved for trading. Below are updates to Cloud allocations for each exchange: MIAX Options Exchange : Market Data Feed content for SPCX will be disseminated across Cloud 22 (previously Cloud 20) multicast addresses for: Top of Market (ToM) Feed Complex Top of Market (cToM) Feed MIAX Order Feed (MOR) Administrative Information Subscriber (AIS) Feed MIAX Express Interface (MEI) customers will need to direct their interface activity for options on SPCX to their MEI sessions on Cloud 22 (previously Cloud 20) FIX Order Interface (FOI), Clearing Trade Drop (CTD) and FIX Drop Copy (FXD) customers are not impacted MIAX Pearl Options and MIAX Emerald Options: Market Data Feed content for SPCX will be disseminated across Cloud 8 (previously Cloud 10) multicast addresses for: Top of Market (ToM) Feed Complex Top of Market (cToM) Feed MIAX Order Feed (MOR) Administrative Information Subscriber (AIS) Feed Pearl Liquidity Feed (PLF) MIAX Express Interface (MEI) and/or MIAX Express Order (MEO) customers will need to direct their interface activity for options on SPCX to their MEI/MEO sessions on Cloud 8 (previously Cloud 10) FIX Order Interface (FOI), Clearing Trade Drop (CTD) and FIX Drop Copy (FXD) customers are not impacted MIAX Sapphire Options : Market Data Feed content for SPCX will be disseminated across Cloud 7 (previously Cloud 2) multicast addresses for: Top of Market (ToM) Feed Complex Top of Market (cToM) Feed Sapphire Liquidity Feed (SLF) MIAX Express Order (MEO) customers will need to direct their interface activity for options on SPCX to their MEO sessions on Cloud 7 (previously Cloud 2) FIX Order Interface (FOI), Clearing Trade Drop (CTD) and FIX Drop Copy (FXD) customers are not impacted In addition to the allocation of symbol SPCX to Cloud 7, MIAX Sapphire Options will also move symbol range (YALA-ZWS) from Cloud 7 to Cloud 6. Effective for trading on Monday, June 15, 2026: Cloud 6: T-Z (with the exception of symbol ZYME) Cloud 7: ZYME (and symbol SPCX once options begin trading) For additional details, please visit MIAX Options Interface Specifications , MIAX Pearl Options Interface Specifications , MIAX Emerald Options Interface Specifications and MIAX Sapphire Options Interface Specifications . If you have any questions, please contact Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302.

title: MIAX Options Exchange - Reminder: Planned Internal Network Switch Refresh, Maintenance and Optional Testing
raw_text:
MIAX Options — Trading Alert, Technical Alert — MIAX Options Exchange - Reminder: Planned Internal Network Switch Refresh, Maintenance and Optional Testing
Trading Alert, Technical Alert MIAX Options June 08, 2026 | 12:47 PM MIAX Options Exchange - Reminder: Planned Internal Network Switch Refresh, Maintenance and Optional Testing As previously announced in the June 2, 2026 Alert , the MIAX Options Exchange will refresh switches and perform internal maintenance on the following dates: Phase 1 - Friday, June 12, 2026, post close trading Phase 2 - Friday, June 26, 2026, post close trading Following completion of the maintenance, firms can perform the following optional verifications on Saturday, June 13, 2026 and Saturday, June 27, 2026: Verify connectivity to the subscribed unicast services (i.e. establish application-level socket connections to MEO, MEI, FOI, CTD, etc., for all ports, across all CCs) Confirm heartbeat receipt for both the A & B market data feeds for all subscribed multicast feeds across each CC Please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302 to arrange testing or to receive additional information on the planned network refresh.

title: MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Reminder: Maintenance 1G LL Shared Extranet Switch Infrastructure
raw_text:
MIAX Pearl — Trading Alert, Technical Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Reminder: Maintenance 1G LL Shared Extranet Switch Infrastructure
Trading Alert, Technical Alert MIAX Pearl June 08, 2026 | 12:44 PM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Reminder: Maintenance 1G LL Shared Extranet Switch Infrastructure As previously announced in the May 13, 2026 Alert , the MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchanges will perform maintenance on the 1G Low Latency (LL) shared extranet switch infrastructure (NY4) after the close of business on Friday, June 12, 2026 between 5:30 p.m. and 11:30 p.m. ET. This maintenance requires no changes by member firms. Connectivity testing will be available for firms to confirm market data heartbeats and market data retransmission services on Saturday, June 13, 2026 between 8:30 a.m. and 9:30 a.m. ET. To coordinate connectivity testing, please contact MIAX Trading Operations prior to Saturday's testing at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com .

title: MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - SPCX Symbol to Cloud Allocation
raw_text:
MIAX Emerald — Technical Alert, Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - SPCX Symbol to Cloud Allocation
Technical Alert, Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert MIAX Emerald June 05, 2026 | 10:34 AM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - SPCX Symbol to Cloud Allocation In preparation for the SpaceX IPO (Symbol SPCX), the MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options, and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchanges will list options on SPCX on the following Clouds: MIAX Options Exchange : Market Data Feed content for SPCX will be disseminated across the Cloud 20 multicast addresses for: Top of Market (ToM) Feed Complex Top of Market (cToM) Feed MIAX Order Feed (MOR) Administrative Information Subscriber (AIS) Feed MIAX Express Interface (MEI) customers will need to direct their interface activity for options on SPCX to their MEI sessions on Cloud 20 FIX Order Interface (FOI), Clearing Trade Drop (CTD) and FIX Drop Copy (FXD) customers are not impacted MIAX Pearl Options and MIAX Emerald Options: Market Data Feed content for SPCX will be disseminated across the Cloud 10 multicast addresses for: Top of Market (ToM) Feed Complex Top of Market (cToM) Feed MIAX Order Feed (MOR) Administrative Information Subscriber (AIS) Feed Pearl Liquidity Feed (PLF) MIAX Express Interface (MEI) and/or MIAX Express Order (MEO) customers will need to direct their interface activity for options on SPCX to their MEI/MEO sessions on Cloud 10 FIX Order Interface (FOI), Clearing Trade Drop (CTD) and FIX Drop Copy (FXD) customers are not impacted MIAX Sapphire Options : Market Data Feed content for SPCX will be disseminated across the Cloud 2 multicast addresses for: Top of Market (ToM) Feed Complex Top of Market (cToM) Feed Sapphire Liquidity Feed (SLF) MIAX Express Order (MEO) customers will need to direct their interface activity for options on SPCX to their MEO sessions on Cloud 2 FIX Order Interface (FOI), Clearing Trade Drop (CTD) and FIX Drop Copy (FXD) customers are not impacted For additional details, please visit MIAX Options Interface Specifications , MIAX Pearl Options Interface Specifications , MIAX Emerald Options Interface Specifications and MIAX Sapphire Options Interface Specifications . If you have any questions, please contact Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302.

title: MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Update: Upcoming Enhancements - Live on June 22 and 25
raw_text:
MIAX Sapphire — Regulatory Alert, Technical Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Update: Upcoming Enhancements - Live on June 22 and 25
Regulatory Alert, Technical Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Sapphire June 04, 2026 | 10:45 AM MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Update: Upcoming Enhancements - Live on June 22 and 25 As previously announced in the May 21, 2026 Alert , the following functionality for the MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange will be available in production on June 22 and 25, 2026 (previously June 11, 2026). Increased Options Leg Ratio - Available Monday, June 22, 2026 This feature supports the increase of the Options leg ratio for Complex orders up to 999,999 Consistent with current handling, The Order Quantity multiplied by the highest Option Leg Ratio of the strategy must be less than or equal to 999,999 The Order Quantity multiplied by the Stock Leg Ratio of the strategy, if present, must be less than or equal to 99,999,999 This enhancement is contained within the FOI Interface (2.3a) release Certification is not required to use this feature in production Selective Liquidity Auto Purge (SLAP) Functionality - Available Thursday, June 25, 2026 This optional feature enhances the Mass Cancellation functionality by providing Members with the ability to remove a subset of its orders in the System With the introduction of a new “SLAP Codes” field in the Standard Binary Order – New Message, Members can specify custom SLAP codes, numbered 1 through 8, on some or all of its liquidity, providing more granular control of the Liquidity Mass Cancel Request when attempting to “Selectively Purge” specific liquidity The SLAP enhancement is contained within the MEO Interface (v1.2) release This feature requires certification prior to being used in production Additional Details These enhancements are currently available in the MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange's Firm Test Bed (FTB2) environment Updated specifications can be found at MIAX Sapphire Options Interface Specifications Please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302 to update configurations, arrange interface testing and schedule certification.

title: MIAX Pearl Options Exchange - Reminder: Planned Network Switch Hardware Refresh for the 10G ULL Extranet and Mandatory Testing
raw_text:
MIAX Pearl — Trading Alert, Technical Alert — MIAX Pearl Options Exchange - Reminder: Planned Network Switch Hardware Refresh for the 10G ULL Extranet and Mandatory Testing
Trading Alert, Technical Alert MIAX Pearl June 03, 2026 | 09:50 AM MIAX Pearl Options Exchange - Reminder: Planned Network Switch Hardware Refresh for the 10G ULL Extranet and Mandatory Testing As previously announced in the May 7, 2026 Alert , MIAX Pearl Options Exchange will refresh customer facing 10G ULL extranet edge switches on Friday, June 5, 2026, after the close of trading. All MIAX Pearl Options Exchange Extranet edge switches will be replaced one to one with the new model, and all firm Cross Connects (CCs) will be moved to the same ports on the new switches. There are no changes required by firms for this hardware upgrade. There are no changes on the 1G LL shared Extranet. Following the upgrade, firms must conduct mandatory testing for all Pearl Options 10G ULL CCs. Details on testing and verifications: Testing will be held on Saturday, June 6, 2026, from 9:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. EDT. Firms are required to perform the following verifications: Verify light levels and IP routing. Verify all 10G ULL CCs by logging into each subscribed application for each CC (i.e. establish application-level socket connections to MEO, MEI, FOI, CTD, etc., for all ports, across all CCs) and verify the login. Confirm heartbeat receipt for both the A & B market data feeds for all subscribed multicast feeds across each CC. After verifying connectivity, firms must inform MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com . Please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302 to arrange testing or to receive additional information on the planned network refresh.

title: MIAX Futures Exchange - Minneapolis Hard Red Spring Wheat - Discontinuance of Warehouse Receipts for Delivery Against Futures
raw_text:
MIAX Futures — Clearing Alert, Product Alert, Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert — MIAX Futures Exchange - Minneapolis Hard Red Spring Wheat - Discontinuance of Warehouse Receipts for Delivery Against Futures
Clearing Alert, Product Alert, Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert MIAX Futures June 18, 2026 | 04:01 PM MIAX Futures Exchange - Minneapolis Hard Red Spring Wheat - Discontinuance of Warehouse Receipts for Delivery Against Futures As previously announced and approved by the CFTC, physical delivery in satisfaction of Minneapolis Hard Red Spring Wheat (HRSW) Futures will change from warehouse receipts to shipping certificates beginning with the September 2026 delivery period. Effective July 24, 2026, for the September 2026 and subsequent contracts, warehouse receipts will not be deliverable against Minneapolis HRSW Futures. Physical delivery in satisfaction of Minneapolis HRSW Futures for September 2026 and subsequent contracts will be by shipping certificate only. After the conclusion of the July 2026 delivery period on July 23, 2026, holders of outstanding warehouse receipts issued prior to the September 2026 contract may: Sell the warehouse receipt back to the issuing warehouse at a mutually agreeable price; Cancel the warehouse receipt for load-out; Continue holding the warehouse receipt and accrue storage obligations to the issuing warehouse; Sell, transfer, or otherwise dispose of such receipts as permitted by the United States Warehouse Act and applicable law. For all contract months up to and through the conclusion of the July 2026 delivery period (July 23, 2026): The maximum load-out charges on delivery grain tendered in satisfaction of a Minneapolis HRSW Futures Contract shall be eight cents (8¢) per bushel for wheat regardless of the date of the warehouse receipt. The maximum storage charges on delivery grain tendered in satisfaction of a Minneapolis HRSW Futures Contract shall be seven cents (7¢) per bushel per month or two thousand three hundred thirty three thousandths of a cent ($.002333) per bushel per day for wheat regardless of the date of the warehouse receipt. Insurance charges shall be included within the maximum storage charges. See Rule 50.3.3 for more information on load-out, storage, and insurance charges. Effective with the September 2026 contract, clearing members will be able to register shipping certificates on behalf of regular elevators for delivery against Minneapolis HRSW Futures. Additional information on shipping certificates can be found in the FAQ here . If you have any questions, please contact MIAX Futures Market Regulation at MIAXFuturesMarketReg@miaxglobal.com . Additional updates will be provided through future Alerts.

title: NYSE - New Security Notification for Symbol ETSS and ETSS WS
raw_text:
NYSE - New Security Notification for Symbol ETSS and ETSS WS
Markets: NYSE; Services: Trading
NYSE has determined that the Class A Ordinary Shares issued by Energy Transition Special Opportunities is part of a new security offering that will trade under the ticker ETSS with CUSIP G23017109 on June 4, 2026. As this new offering has not previously traded on any listing market and has no prior day's closing price, Regulation SHO Rule 201 will not apply to security ETSS until its second day of trading on NYSE. Further, NYSE Rule 123D(d) will apply and the security will be in a regulatory halt until the NYSE has opened trading. NYSE has determined that the Warrants, each whole warrant exercisable for one Class A ordinary share at an exercise price of $11.50 issued by Energy Transition Special Opportunities is part of a new security offering that will trade under the ticker ETSS WS with CUSIP G23017117 on June 4, 2026. As this new offering has not previously traded on any listing market and has no prior day's closing price, Regulation SHO Rule 201 will not apply to security ETSS WS until its second day of trading on NYSE. Further, NYSE Rule 123D(d) will apply and the security will be in a regulatory halt until the NYSE has opened trading.

title: Welcome to the New IEX Trading Alert and System Status Application – IEX Options Alerts
raw_text:
Welcome to the New IEX Trading Alert and System Status Application – IEX Options Alerts
Category: Informational; Alert 2026-004
Thank you again for subscribing to updates and future trading alerts from IEX Options. Welcome to the IEX Trading Alert and System Status Application. If you are receiving this via email: You have been subscribed to all Options Trading Alerts as well as all System Status Updates. To update your preferences or add a number to receive SMS text alerts, please go to https://notifications.iex.io and complete the steps below. (If you do not make updates, you will continue to be subscribed to Options Trading Alerts and Options System Status Updates, and will receive them in the format you received this notification.) Click on the Log In button in the top right side of the screen. Enter the email address that you would like to subscribe to receive alerts. Look for an email with the subject line “Your Magic Link for IEX Trading Alerts” with a link to log in and update your preferences. Clicking on the link or copying and pasting the link directly into your browser will automatically log you in and allow you to update your preferences through the “My Preferences” tab, which will be available once logged in. The “My Preferences” tab is where you can update the email address you receive alerts to as well as the phone number to receive SMS notifications. If you would like to subscribe additional or new email addresses to receive alerts, you can do so at https://notifications.iex.io : Under “Trading Alerts,” click “Subscribe.” Enter the email address that you would like to subscribe receive alerts. Look for an email with the subject line “Your Magic Link for IEX Trading Alerts” with a link to log in and update your preferences. Clicking on the link or copying and pasting the link directly into your browser will automatically log you in and allow you to update your preferences through the “My Preferences” tab, which will be available once logged in. We recommend checking your spam or junk folder if you do not see the message in your inbox. More Information Please contact Market Operations or Sales with any questions: Market Operations: optionsmktops@iextrading.com Sales: sales@iextrading.com About IEX Exchange Investors’ Exchange LLC (IEX Exchange) is a national securities exchange designed to provide superior execution quality and set a new standard for trading. Since launching in 2016, the exchange has grown to be one of the largest exchange operators globally, by notional value traded. In 2026, IEX plans to launch IEX Options, a new U.S. options exchange that will bring its suite of order protection innovations designed to better address the needs of market makers and drive better outcomes for end investors. To learn more visit iex.io .

IEX Exchange Launches Channelized DEEP+ Testing in ITF Environment on June 3, 2026
Category: Market Data and Connectivity; Alert 2026-002
Alert #2026-002 has been updated to revise the tables under "DEEP+ ITF Channelization Assignment and Multicast Configuration." Overview On June 3, 2026, IEX Exchange is making channelized DEEP+ available in IEX’s test environment (ITF). As mentioned in Trading Alert #2026-014 , DEEP+ Subscribers may test channelized DEEP+ functionality, including DEEP+ Snapshot and Retransmission features, in the ITF environment to ensure their systems are prepared for the launch of channelized DEEP+, at a soon-to-be announced date. IEX has updated DEEP+ and DEEP+ Snapshot specification documents to reflect relevant connectivity and functionality changes. No changes are expected for Subscribers who wish to continue receiving the current non-channelized DEEP+ feed. Details IEX requests that DEEP+ Subscribers leveraging channelized DEEP+ certify in ITF prior to connecting to production. Subscribers are requested to ensure proper ingestion of channelized DEEP+ messages along with functionality for Snapshot and Retransmission based on the updated specifications. The following tables detail the symbol channelization split along with multicast configuration to connect to the ITF environment. IEX reserves the right to add multicast addresses or change symbol channel assignments with at least two business days' prior notice to Subscribers. DEEP+ ITF Channelization Assignment and Multicast Configuration ChannelID Symbol Start Range* Port ITF Multicast ITF Snapshot 1 A 13337 233.215.21.245 23.226.155.29 2 K 13338 3 - 13339 4 - 13340 5 - 13341 6 - 13342 7 - 13343 8 - 13344 9 - 13345 10 - 13346 11 - 13347 12 - 13348 13 - 13349 14 - 13350 15 - 13351 16 - 13352 *Unassigned symbol channels 3 through 16 will have heartbeat messages published over them. ChannelID Symbol Start Range Port (UDP) ITF Retransmission (UDP) Port (TCP) ITF Retransmission (TCP) 1 A 23337 23.226.155.27 33337 23.226.155.26 2 K 23338 33338 3 - 23339 33339 4 - 23340 33340 5 - 23341 33341 6 - 23342 33342 7 - 23343 33343 8 - 23344 33344 9 - 23345 33345 10 - 23346 33346 11 - 23347 33347 12 - 23348 33348 13 - 23349 33349 14 - 23350 33350 15 - 23351 33351 16 - 23352 33352 Channelized Snapshot and Retransmission Request Example The current DEEP+ Snapshot and Retransmission functionality currently supports channelization requests today. To date, the ChannelID value that has been sent on Snapshot and Retransmission Request messages has been 1, a single channel value. Going forward, the ChannelID value will now represent the symbol split channel assignments for the respective production, ITF and disaster recovery environments for channelized DEEP+. Non-channelized DEEP+ will continue to use a ChannelID value of 1. For example, in ITF, if a DEEP+ Snapshot request is for symbol NVDA, that symbol will be on ChannelID 2 and the subsequent SnapshotRequest Message must have the correct ChannelID value of 2. Channelized DEEP+ Snapshot Request Field Name Offset Length Type Description/Notes Message Length 0 2 Integer Length of message in bytes not including this field (57) Message Type 2 1 Byte ‘r’ (0x72) – SnapshotRequest Authentication Token 3 40 Byte Token supplied by IEX Market Ops. Left justified string space padded on right. ChannelID 43 4 Integer Channel Identifier. (1 – 16) SessionID 47 4 Integer Session Identifier Minimum Sequence Number 51 8 Long Minimum Sequence Number useable by client. Channelized DEEP+ Retransmission Request Field Name Offset Length Type Description/Notes Version 0 1 Byte 1 – Version of Transport specification Request Type 1 1 Byte 1 – Sequenced Messages 2 – Bytestream Message Protocol ID 2 2 Short Unique identifier of the higher-layer protocol ChannelID 4 4 Integer Channel Identifier. (1 – 16) SessionID 8 4 Integer Identifies the session Request Range Count 12 4 Integer Number of ranges requested Testing Opportunities in ITF Please see the IEX Connectivity Manua l and Physical Connectivity Order Form for information about accessing the ITF via a dedicated cross-connect. Questions related to this should be directed to IEX Market Operations . The ITF environment is accessible Monday through Friday from 9:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. ET. Additional Resources IEX DEEP+ Specification IEX DEEP+ SNAP Specification IEX Transport Specification IEX Market Data and Connectivity More Information Please contact ITF Support , IEX Market Operations or Sales with any questions: ITF Support: itfsupport@iextrading.com Market Operations: marketops@iextrading.com Sales: sales@iextrading.com About IEX Exchange Investors’ Exchange LLC (IEX Exchange) is a national securities exchange designed to provide superior execution quality and set a new standard for trading. Since launching in 2016, the exchange has grown to be one of the largest exchange operators globally, by notional value traded. To learn more about IEX Exchange visit iex.io .

title: UPDATE: Extension of OPRA Trading Hours
raw_text:
UPDATE: Extension of OPRA Trading Hours
June 15, 2026 To: OPRA Multicast Subscribers Subject: Extension of OPRA Trading Hours - Update What you need to know: In a previous announcement, OPRA announced that it would begin supporting trading hours from 7:30am to 5:00pm as of July 13, 2026. The implementation date has now been revised to August 17, 2026, as described in this Participant notice. OPRA will support industry testing for the extension of hours as detailed in the Participant notice. Who is impacted? All OPRA Data Subscribers who receive the OPRA feeds are impacted by this change. How can I obtain further information? For business inquiries refer to the contact information in Participant Notice above. For OPRA inquiries: • SIAC Product Management Support Email: CTA-OPRA-Support@siac.com • SIAC Product Management Support Line: 212-656-8177, Option 2 (Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM-5:00 PM ET) Securities Industry Automation Corporation 11 Wall Street, New York, NY 10005 Securities Industry Automation Corporation 11 Wall Street, New York, NY 10005

DTN2026-9 - LendingClub Corporation to Begin Listing and Trading on Nasdaq on June 22, 2026
raw_text:
DTN2026-9 - LendingClub Corporation to Begin Listing and Trading on Nasdaq on June 22, 2026
News CategoryAlertMarketsWhat You Need To Know
Data Technical News#2026-009The Nasdaq Stock Market

On June 22, LendingClub Corporation (NYSE: LC) is expected to be listed and begin trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the new name and ticker symbol Happen Inc. (HAPN).

Nasdaq requests that market data redistributors update their databases to reflect the new listing market for Happen Inc., (HAPN) after market close on June 18th and maintain historical data for LC prior to transfer date.

title: Weekend Testing Dates
raw_text:
Weekend Testing Dates — BOX circular 2026-059 — announcements
2026-059 Notice 2026-059 [1 of 1] Weekend Testing Dates TO: BOX Options Participants and Data Recipients FROM: BOX Market Operations Center SUBJECT: Weekend Testing Dates DATE: June 16, 2026 Effective immediately, BOX Options Market LLC (“BOX”) will support regular weekend testing for all BOX Participants and Data Recipients. A complete list of all weekend testing dates and timeframes can be found on the BOX Website. Overview To support ongoing system readiness, performance validation, and operational reliability for both the Exchange and its Participants and Data Recipients, BOX will host a series of weekend testing dates for its trading system and related components. The following BOX services will be available during this time: • FIX Order Entry • SAIL Order Entry • SAIL Quote Entry • SAIL Quote Purge • Automated Trade Reporting (“ATR”) • High Speed Vendor Feed (“HSVF”) Market Data BOX Participants and Data Recipients may leverage their existing connections at Equinix NY4 for access to the weekend testing windows. These dates and timeframes will be unstaffed unless otherwise communicated. Contact Please contact the BOX Market Operations Center at (866) 768-8845 or by e-mail at BOXMOC@boxoptions.com should you require additional information.

title: Optimization of Implied Market Data Dissemination
raw_text:
Optimization of Implied Market Data Dissemination — BOX circular 2026-055 — announcements, technical notices
2026-055 Notice 2026-055 [1 of 2] Optimization of Implied Market Data Dissemination TO: BOX Options Participants and Data Recipients FROM: BOX Market Operations Center SUBJECT: Optimization of Implied Market Data Dissemination DATE: June 11, 2026 Overview Currently, Implied Orders are generated for Strategies with two legs and with a ratio of one-to- one. The system generates and disseminates, via the High Speed Vendor Feed (“HSVF”), Implied Order pricing, regardless of whether there is an order on the Complex Order Book for that Strategy. Effective July 13, 2026, the system will compute and disseminate Implied Order pricing for Strategies with two legs and with a ratio of one-to-one only if there is an order on the Complex Order Book for that Strategy. What does it mean for Participants and Data Recipients? With this optimization, the system will only generate and disseminate an Implied Order price for Strategies with two legs and with a ratio of one-to-one if an order is submitted to the Complex Order Book on that Strategy. Consequently, Participants and Data Recipients will see fewer Implied Orders being disseminated on the HSVF. Additional Considerations This change does not impact priority or order matching under Rule 7240. Additionally, there will be no change to how the system handles Strategies with more than two legs or with a ratio other than one-to-one. 2026-055 Notice 2026-055 [2 of 2] Contact Please review the information provided herein. If you have any questions regarding this information, please contact the BOX Market Operations Center at BOXMOC@boxoptions.com.

title: OPRA Notice of Trading Session Identifier
raw_text:
OPRA Notice of Trading Session Identifier
June 22, 2026 To: OPRA Multicast Subscribers Subject: New Trading Session Identifier Field What you need to know: OPRA is introducing a new Trading Session Identifier field to allow Participants to identify in which session a trade took place. Background: The OPRA Last Sale message supports a single Sale Condition in the Message Type field from a full suite of values. Participants currently utilize the sale condition ‘v’ to denote Extended Trading Hours (ETH) trades, which prevents use of the other available sale conditions that data recipients rely upon to identify the type of trade that took place. With the introduction of ETH in OPRA, the ability to identify session independently of sale condition is required. The proposed change addresses this limitation. Until implementation of this enhancement, it is anticipated Participants will use the Sale Condition ‘v’ - Extended Hours Trade, for all trades outside of regular trading hours. Sale Condition 'v' will be deprecated transitionally and retired once all ETH Participants have migrated to use the new Trading Session Identifier field, date is to be determined. What is Changing? New Trading Session Identifier Field — An existing Reserved byte in the Last Sale message is being repurposed as a single-byte Trading Session Identifier. The reserved byte is currently transmitted as zero ‘0’ (Hex 0x00) by all Participants. The new field (1 Byte, unsigned integer) will allow Participants to signal the applicable trading session independently of the Sale Condition field, enabling both to be conveyed simultaneously. Since zero ‘0’ maps to Regular Session and is already the default, this change is backwards compatible and non-ETH Participants do not need to make any change. Field Values Value Session Notes 0 Regular Trading Session Default — current state for all Participants; no change required 1 Extended Trading Hours To be populated by ETH Participants Securities Industry Automation Corporation 11 Wall Street, New York, NY 10005 Securities Industry Automation Corporation 11 Wall Street, New York, NY 10005 2 Who is impacted? All OPRA Data Subscribers who receive the OPRA feeds are impacted by this change. OPRA will support this change beginning Monday, September 21, 2026, at which time ETH Participants may begin populating the new Trading Session Identifier. OPRA Data Subscribers will only see the new Trading Session value of ‘1’ when OPRA Participants are ready to generate it, which could be at a later date and should be prepared to continue to process the ‘v’ Sale Condition until it is retired. The date of playback test data availability will be communicated in a follow-up notice. Documentation: Please reference the revised OPRA Output Multicast Specifications which may be obtained from www.opraplan.com. OPRA Notifications To automatically receive these notifications and OPRA and GTH system alerts, you are required to subscribe at: https://www.opraplan.com/contact-us How can I obtain further information? For technical OPRA inquiries: • SIAC Product Management Support Email: CTA-OPRA-Support@siac.com • SIAC Product Management Support Line: 212-656-8177, Option 2 (Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM-5:00 PM ET)

Listing Alert MIAX Emerald June 23, 2026 | 05:04 PM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: DuPont de Nemours, Inc. (DD) DuPont de Nemours, Inc. (DD) announced a reverse split effective for Wednesday, June 24, 2026. The MIAX Options Exchange, MIAX Pearl Options Exchange, MIAX Emerald Options Exchange and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange will not list DD2 options. Therefore, no options on DD will be available for trading on Wednesday, June 24, 2026. DuPont de Nemours, Inc. (DD) will resume trading on the MIAX Options Exchange, MIAX Pearl Options Exchange, MIAX Emerald Options Exchange and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange on Thursday, June 25, 2026. All GTC orders resting on the MIAX order books in DD will be canceled at the close of business on Tuesday, June 23, 2026. Regulatory Alert, Clearing Alert MIAX Futures June 23, 2026 | 02:30 PM MIAX Futures Exchange - Offsets and Positions Reporting - July 2026 Futures The deadline to report offsets of July 2026 Minneapolis Hard Red Spring Wheat futures is 7:30 a.m. CT on Monday, June 29, 2026, for the Friday, June 26, 2026, trade date. After the deadline, offsets of July 2026 futures may only be made via same-day trade activity . Full details can be found in the attached memo. Please note that July 2026 futures are the last delivery month for which warehouse receipts will be deliverable against Minneapolis Hard Red Spring Wheat futures. Effective with the September 2026 delivery month, delivery will be by shipping certificate only. More information is available in the alert here . If you have any questions, please contact the MIAX Futures Market Regulation Department at MIAXFuturesMarketReg@MIAXGlobal.com . Trading Alert, Technical Alert MIAX Futures June 23, 2026 | 11:47 AM MIAX Futures Exchange - Commodity and Financial - Onyx Trading Platform - Mandatory Upgrades & Updated Interface Specifications to Support Q4 2026 Enhancements The MIAX Futures Exchange has published updated Interface Specifications for the MIAX Futures Onyx Trading Platform to support upcoming enhancements for: Commodity Futures (CH4) on the evening of September 27, 2026 for the September 28, 2026 trade date Financial Futures (NY5) in Q4 2026, final date to be announced via a future alert All enhancements related to the Depth of Market (DoM) and Top of Market (ToM) Feed will be mandatory. Firms will be required to cut over to the updated protocol version on the day that the functionality is live on the platform. A MIAX Futures Onyx 2026 Release Guide has been published on the website and is also attached to this Alert. New Features Commodity Futures : Market-Limit Order Market Order with Protection Stop Market Order with Protection Complex Settlement Price EOD Dissemination Reserve Order Trading at Settlement (TAS) Cross Product Spreads Active/Inactive Instrument demarcation on Market Data to denote new upcoming Products as “Inactive” Financial Futures : Reserve Order Basis Trade at Index Close (BTIC) Cross Product Spreads Active/Inactive Instrument demarcation on Market Data to denote new upcoming Products as “Inactive” Important Dates: Commodity Futures (CH4) FTB2 Test Area Enhancements will be available week of June 29, 2026 Financial Futures (NY5) FTB2 Test Area Enhancements will be available week of June 29, 2026 Commodity Futures (CH4) Enhancement Launch on the evening of September 27, 2026 for the September 28, 2026 trade date Financial Futures (NY5) Enhancement Launch in Q4 2026, final date to be announced via a future alert Updated Interface Specifications: Depth of Market (DoM) Feed – Version 1.3 (Mandatory Upgrade) Updated Simple Instrument Definition including new fields and values Replaced legacy Complex Instrument Definition with new, expanded version of same message Added new values to existing fields in multiple messages Top of Market (ToM) Feed – Version 1.3 (Mandatory Upgrade) Updated Simple Instrument Definition including new fields and values Replaced legacy Complex Instrument Definition with new, expanded version of same message Added new values to existing fields in multiple messages FIX Order Interface Specification (FOI) – Version 1.3 (Optional Upgrade) Updated New Order - Single to include new fields Updated Order Cancel/Replace Request to include new field Updated Execution Report including new fields and values Updated Security Definition with new values Updated Trade Cancel/Correct with new values Updated Order Mass Cancel Request with new values Updated FXD Security Definition including new fields and values Futures Express Interface Specification (FEI) – Version 1.3 (Optional Upgrade) Updated New Order Request including new fields and values Added new reject reasons to New Order Response and Modify Order Response Updated Modify Order Request to include new field Updated New Order Notification including new fields and values Updated Modify Order Notification to include new field Added new message Reserve Order Replenishment Notification Updated Simple Execution Notification to include new field Updated Complex Execution Notification to include new field Replaced legacy Mass Cancel Request with new, expanded version of same message Updated Strategy Creation Response to remove an error code MIAX Futures Onyx Resources: Please visit the MIAX Futures Onyx site for the most recent information Onyx Interface Specifications Onyx Functionality Onyx Trading Connectivity Onyx Market Data & Vendor Agreements Onyx Approved Vendors Onyx Audit Trail Requirements Onyx Trading Platform Access & Preferred Rate Program Onyx Listings Onyx Product Settings MIAX Futures Fees For additional information, please contact MIAX Futures Trading Operations at MIAXFuturesTradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7303. title: NYSE - 2026 Russell Reconstitution
raw_text:
NYSE - 2026 Russell Reconstitution
Markets: NYSE, NYSE ARCA EQUITIES; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
Russell Reconstitution - June 26, 2026 Overview The newly reconstituted Russell indices (Russell Global Index, Russell 1000® Index, Russell 2000® Index Russell 3000® Index and Russell Microcap® Index) will take effect on Monday, June 29, 2026 , and will remain in place for the next 6-months. The previous indices will cease being in effect after the close of the U.S. equity markets on Friday, June 26, 2026. FTSE Russell will use the NYSE Official Closing Prices to price NYSE-listed securities added to the indices. Please review the FTSE Russell webpage for updates and final lists of additions and deletions. Normal Market on Close/Limit on Close (MOC/LOC) rules and procedures will apply on June 26, 2026. The NYSE would like to remind participants of various system interruption contingencies as we head into the Russell Reconstitution. Communications NYSE will use its standard system status communication methods - www.nyse.com/market-status/history , SMS and email - to disseminate event updates and inform market participants of any unusual market conditions. To subscribe or update preferences for NYSE Market Status Notices, visit www.nyse.com/subscriptions . Contingency Procedures If the NYSE experiences a system disruption that would impact its ability to conduct a closing auction, it will execute one of the following contingency scenarios: System Interruption Contingency Scenario 1 (At or Before 3:00 PM) If after exhausting all recovery options, the NYSE determines at or before 3:00 p.m. ET that it is unable to conduct a closing transaction in one or more securities, the NYSE will designate NYSE Arca as the alternate exchange. Consequently, the NYSE's Official Closing Price in the affected securities will be the official closing price under the rules of NYSE Arca. In such an event, all closing interest on the NYSE in the affected securities will be deemed cancelled and member organizations will be encouraged to re-route their closing interest for participation in the NYSE Arca closing process. System Interruption Contingency Scenario 2 (After 3:00PM) If an NYSE system interruption occurs after 3:00 p.m. ET whereby service will not be restored, the NYSE's Official Closing Price will be the VWAP of the last consolidated sale prices for the last five minutes of trading for each affected NYSE-listed security. The NYSE will notify market participants via the market-wide call and a system status posting that the closing process will not occur and that all closing interest on the NYSE in the affected securities will be deemed cancelled. Questions about this memo should be directed to your Relationship Manager or rmteam@nyse.com . NYSE Trading Operations Relationship Management Technology Member Services Client Relationship Services Connectivity/Order Routing Support Trading@nyse.com RMTeam@nyse.com Tms@nyse.com CRS@nyse.com Support@nyse.com 212-896-2830 x1 212-896-2830 x3 212 896 2830 x2

title: 2026 Regulation SCI BCP/DR Testing - Saturday, October 24, 2026
raw_text:
2026 Regulation SCI BCP/DR Testing - Saturday, October 24, 2026
Category: Regulatory; Alert 2026-006
Overview In accordance with Regulation Systems Compliance and Integrity (" Reg SCI "), IEX Exchange will conduct testing of its Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Plans ("BCP/DR") during the planned SIFMA industry-wide test ("SIFMA BCP Test") on Saturday, October 24, 2026. IEX Exchange encourages all Members to participate in the SIFMA BCP Test and welcomes any firms to contact IEX Market Operations to establish connectivity to the Disaster Recovery ("DR") environment for the test. However, certain Members are designated as mandatory participants pursuant to the Designation Criteria outlined below. At this time, all mandatory participants have been notified by IEX Exchange. IEX Exchange strongly encourages mandatory participants to take part in at least one IEX Exchange-supported pre-test scheduled on Saturday, September 26 and October 10 to ensure that their connectivity will be operational for the 2026 SIFMA BCP Test on October 24. Please review the 2026 IEX Test Script for details on the 2026 SIFMA BCP Test. Designation Criteria Pursuant to IEX Rule 2.250 and as set forth in IEX Regulatory Circular 2025 - 0005 , IEX has designated Members that account for three (3) percent or more of executed volume on IEX, measured on a quarterly basis, as required to connect to IEX’s backup system in Chicago and participate in mandatory BCP/DR testing. Members that received a notice of designation in the third or fourth quarter of 2025 (pertaining to trading in the second or third quarter of 2025, respectively) and did not participate in the 2025 test, as well as Members that received a notice of designation in the first or second quarter of 2026 (pertaining to trading in fourth quarter of 2025 and first quarter 2026, respectively) are mandatory participants in this test. DR Environment Connectivity Members should contact IEX Market Operations to establish connectivity to the DR environment in one of two ways: via cross connect: Members must complete and submit a Physical Connectivity Order Form to IEX Market Operations . via extranet: Members must first contact their extranet provider to arrange connectivity and then IEX Market Operations for the target IP address. See the IEX Connectivity Manual for additional details on IEX Exchange’s approved extranet and telecommunications providers. Participants will not require additional sessions for the DR environment; however, specific IP addresses are assigned for the DR environment. DR Environment The DR environment supports all matching and routing functionality offered by the Exchange. Unlike the primary trading environment (where direct feeds are utilized), the DR environment utilizes the Securities Information Processors ("SIPs") for quotation data. For additional information regarding the DR environment and establishing connectivity, please review IEX Rule 11.510 and the IEX Connectivity Manual . The DR environment is located at the Equinix (CH4) data center, 350 E. Cermak Road, Chicago, IL. Additional Resources SIFMA BCP Test IEX Regulatory Circular 2025 - 0005 IEX Rule 2.250 2026 IEX Test Script Contact Information Please contact IEX Market Operations or Sales with any questions: Market Operations: marketops@iextrading.com Sales: sales@iextrading.com About IEX Exchange Investors’ Exchange LLC (IEX Exchange) is a national securities exchange designed to provide superior execution quality and set a new standard for trading. Since launching in 2016, the exchange has grown to be one of the largest exchange operators globally, by notional value traded. In 2026, IEX plans to launch IEX Options, a new U.S. options exchange that will bring its suite of order protection innovations designed to better address the needs of market makers and drive better outcomes for end investors. To learn more visit iex.io .

title: MIAX Futures Exchange - Spring Wheat Options Expiration - July 2026
raw_text:
MIAX Futures — Clearing Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Futures Exchange - Spring Wheat Options Expiration - July 2026
Clearing Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Futures June 25, 2026 | 03:53 PM MIAX Futures Exchange - Spring Wheat Options Expiration - July 2026 Expiration of July Spring Wheat option positions is scheduled for Friday, June 26, 2026. All Clearing Members should use the MIAX Futures Clearing System (MCS) to update and verify July Spring Wheat option positions by 4:30 PM CT. Clearing Members must submit position information by one of the following: Position Change Submission (PCS) file via SFTP PCS Reporting Specification can be found under Technical Specifications at MIAX Futures Interface Specifications Manually submitting positions to the MIAX Futures Clearing House and verifying in MCS by utilizing the gross position reporting and marking complete. Exception forms to exercise out-of-the money positions or not exercise in-the-money positions, should be submitted by 4:25 PM CT. Firms should expect to see assignments at approximately 5:10 PM CT on June 26, 2026. All account information can be added once option trades have been created. All account information must be added and completed by 7:30 AM CT on Monday, June 29, 2026. For additional information regarding the reporting process, please refer to the MIAX Futures Clearing System User Manual and instructional videos available at MIAX Futures Clearing Training Resources For more information, please contact MIAX Futures Clearing Operations at MIAXFuturesClearingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (612) 321-7146. To learn more about MIAX Futures, please contact MIAXFutures@miaxglobal.com . To register or revise MIAX Futures Alert subscriptions, visit MIAX Email Subscriptions .

title: NYSE - New Security Notification for Symbol OTAI and OTAI RT
raw_text:
NYSE - New Security Notification for Symbol OTAI and OTAI RT
Markets: NYSE; Services: Trading
NYSE has determined that the Ordinary Shares issued by Starlink AI Acquisition Corporation is part of a new security offering that will trade under the ticker OTAI with CUSIP G8443M102 on June 29, 2026. As this new offering has not previously traded on any listing market and has no prior day's closing price, Regulation SHO Rule 201 will not apply to security OTAI until its second day of trading on NYSE. Further, NYSE Rule 123D(d) will apply and the security will be in a regulatory halt until the NYSE has opened trading. NYSE has determined that the Rights, each entitling the holder to receive one-fourth (1/4) of one ordinary share issued by Starlink AI Acquisition Corporation is part of a new security offering that will trade under the ticker OTAI RT with CUSIP G8443M110 on June 29, 2026. As this new offering has not previously traded on any listing market and has no prior day's closing price, Regulation SHO Rule 201 will not apply to security OTAI RT until its second day of trading on NYSE. Further, NYSE Rule 123D(d) will apply and the security will be in a regulatory halt until the NYSE has opened trading.

title: NYSE Arca Options - Disaster Recovery Hardware Upgrade and New Market Data Channel Rebalance
raw_text:
NYSE Arca Options - Disaster Recovery Hardware Upgrade and New Market Data Channel Rebalance
Markets: NYSE ARCA OPTIONS; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
On Monday, June 29, 2026, NYSE Arca Options will upgrade hardware for its Disaster Recovery ("DR") environment. The final industry test for the DR upgrade will take place on Saturday, June 27, 2026, previously announced . On Tuesday, June 30, 2026, the Exchange will add Production symbols on its new, expanded 17 - 24 channels for its proprietary market data feeds. Please refer to the NYSE Proprietary Data IP Addresses file for new channel information. Customers should use the start of day symbol reference data for symbol mapping information. There will be no XDP format changes. Please contact rmteam@nyse.com with any questions regarding this update.

title: Upcoming Market-Wide Circuit Breaker (MWCB) Testing Opportunity on Saturday, July 25, 2026
raw_text:
Upcoming Market-Wide Circuit Breaker (MWCB) Testing Opportunity on Saturday, July 25, 2026
Category: Regulatory; Alert 2026-007
Overview Members are advised that the Securities Information Processors (SIPs) will conduct industry testing of data center failover and Market-Wide Circuit Breaker ("MWCB") Breach Levels 1 and 2 (7% breach and 13% breach) on Saturday, July 25, 2026. In coordination with other market participants, IEX Exchange will participate in the test, and IEX systems will be open from 8:30 a.m. - 12:00 p.m. ET. All Members are encouraged to participate and test their ability to receive messages associated with MWCBs. Designated Members Pursuant to IEX Rule 11.280(i) , Members who meet the designation criteria for Mandatory Participation in Testing of Backup Systems outlined in paragraph (b) of IEX Rule 2.250 (“Designated Members”) are required to participate in at least one industry-wide MWCB test each year. The Testing Year consists of the four quarters immediately following the quarter in which the Exchange determined that a Member contributed a meaningful percentage of the Exchange’s overall volume as set forth in IEX Rule 2.250 . The 2026 Testing Year will be from April 1, 2026 through March 31, 2027. At this time, all Designated Members have been notified by IEX Exchange. Test Requirements & Attestation During the MWCB test, Designated Members are required to complete the following test scenarios: Receive and process MWCB halt messages from the SIPs. Receive and process resume messages from the SIP following a MWCB halt. Receive and process market data from the SIP relevant to the MWCB halt. Send orders following a Level 1 or Level 2 MWCB halt in a manner consistent with their usual trading behavior. After the MWCB test is concluded, Designated Members who participated in the MWCB test are required to complete this attestation and return it to marketops@iextrading.com. Note: Designated Members are required to participate in the industry-wide test but are not specifically required to connect to IEX Exchange as long as their participation satisfies items (1) - (4) above. Testing Opportunities In 2026, the SIPs have established the following Saturday testing schedule for SIP Failovers and Market-Wide Circuit Breakers: Saturday, April 11 (Failover; MWCB Level 1 & 2 Test #1) Saturday, July 25 (Failover; MWCB Level 1 & 2 Test #2) Saturday, September 12 (Dedicated MWCB Level 1, 2 & 3) Saturday, December 5 (Failover; MWCB Level 1 & 2 Test #3) More Information IEX Regulatory Circular 2026-0002 SEC Approval Order UTP Notice Contact Information Please contact IEX Market Operations or Sales with any questions: Market Operations: marketops@iextrading.com Sales: sales@iextrading.com About The Investors Exchange Investors’ Exchange LLC (IEX Exchange) is a national securities exchange designed to provide superior execution quality and set a new standard for trading. Since launching in 2016, the exchange has grown to be one of the largest exchange operators globally, by notional value traded. In 2026, IEX plans to launch IEX Options, a new U.S. options exchange that will bring its suite of order protection innovations designed to better address the needs of market makers and drive better outcomes for end investors. To learn more visit iex.io .

title: Testing Opportunity - OPRA and GTH Failover to Secondary Data Center Testing: Saturday, July 25, 2026, Reminder
raw_text:
Testing Opportunity - OPRA and GTH Failover to Secondary Data Center Testing: Saturday, July 25, 2026, Reminder
1 June 26, 2026 TO: OPRA and OPRA Global Trading Hours (GTH) Multicast Data Subscribers SUBJECT: Testing Opportunity - OPRA and GTH Failover to Secondary Data Center Testing: Saturday, July 25, 2026, Reminder Failover Testing As previously announced, SIAC will conduct OPRA and GTH failover testing to the secondary data center, as well as Market-Wide Circuit Breaker (MWCB) Breach Level 1 and 2 (7% breach and 13% breach) testing with the OPRA exchanges on Saturday, July 25, 2026, from 8:30 am - 11:00 am ET. In addition, OPRA GTH will also conduct failover testing to the secondary data center, from 7:30 am - 9:15 am ET. All OPRA and GTH Multicast Data Subscribers are invited to participate in these tests to validate expected processing within your systems. Remaining 2026 SIP Failover/MWCB Saturday Testing Schedule • Sat 9/12/26: MWCB Level 1/2/3 Test • Sat 12/12/26: SIP Failover / MWCB Level 1/2 Test #3 (revised date) Failover Test Details The GTH failover to the secondary data center can occur at any time during the 7:55 am - 8:15am ET time window, with the OPRA failover occurring at any time during the 10:00 am - 11:00 am ET time window, to simulate an unexpected real-time event requiring a failover. Participating exchanges will continue to generate test data, which will be disseminated over the production multicast lines sourced from the secondary data center. Upon failover to the secondary data center OPRA and GTH Data Subscribers will see: Securities Industry Automation Corporation 11 Wall Street, New York, NY 10005 Securities Industry Automation Corporation 11 Wall Street, New York, NY 10005 2 OPRA and GTH Failover Messaging Description Category H, Type K - Reset Block Sequence Number Control messages to ‘1’ (10x) Category H, Type P - Disaster Recovery Data Center Activation Control messages Zero quotes for all securities OPRA trades and quotes after failover from participating Exchanges Test Registration Each OPRA and GTH Data Subscriber participating in the test should register at CTA- OPRA-Support@siac.com . Test plans are below. Data Subscribers who receive OPRA and GTH from connectivity service providers other than ICE Global Network (IGN) or the NMS Network must contact their connectivity service providers to coordinate testing. Documentation Please reference the latest OPRA and GTH Output Multicast Specifications which may be obtained from www.opraplan.com. OPRA and GTH Notifications To automatically receive these notifications and OPRA and GTH system alerts, you are required to subscribe at: https://www.opraplan.com/contact-us 3 # Time Test Category Action By Test Description Expected Results Friday, July 24 1 7:45:00 PM - 8:30:00 PM SOD OPRA OPRA Global Trading Hours (GTH) to trigger Start of Day message CBOE to establish connectivity for OPRA GTH Input lines CBOE establish connections CBOE to start submitting Quote and Trade data Quotes and trades accepted and disseminated via the multicast output lines Saturday, July 25 3 7:30:00 - 7:45:00 AM Output Publication Primary Process Failure OPRA OPRA GTH to simulate Primary Output Publication process failure. Data Publication to switch to backup process -- Subscribers may see a momentary outage in data flow on the output lines -- Subscriber can request retransmission for any gaps OPRA OPRA GTH to simulate primary input line failure of a subset of OPRA input lines for CBOE CBOE will see primary input lines disconnected CBOE For the affected lines, CBOE to reconnect to OPRA GTH on their backup lines and establish sequence numbers by sending Block Sequence Number Status Inquiry Request (Category N / Type L) and Message Count Status Inquiry Request (Category N / Type R). CBOE to start submitting Quote and Trade data -- OPRA GTH to accept input connections and respond with Block Sequence Response (Category N / Type M) and Message Count Status Response (Category N / Type S) -- Quotes and trades accepted and disseminated on the output lines OPRA GTH to simulate Production site failure -- Disconnection of all Input and Retransmission lines to OPRA GTH -- Disruption in output multicast data dissemination for all lines OPRA GTH to failover to DR site and publish following messages: 1) Reset Block Sequence Number (Category H Type K) for each OPRA output line to reset the sequence number to 1 2) Disaster Recovery Data Center Activation (Category H Type P) message 3) Zero Quotes (Quote messages with Zero Price and Size) on behalf of CBOE across all symbols -- Subscribers to receive and process sequence reset message (Category H / Type K) disseminated from the DR site -- Subscribers to receive the Disaster Recovery Site Activation Control Message (Category H, Type P) over the multicast lines -- Subscribers to receive the zero quotes disseminated upon failover Note: To request retransmission for any range of messages disseminated after the sequence reset to 1, Data Subscriber would be required to apply the offset up to 4,294,967,295 in the message sequence number OPRA GTH to enable input lines and Retransmission lines of the DR site DR Input IP/Ports available to establish connections CBOE to reconnect to input IP/ports on OPRA GTH DR site and establish sequence numbers by sending Block Sequence Number Status Inquiry Request (Category N / Type L) and Message Count Status Inquiry Request (Category N / Type R). OPRA GTH to accept input connections and respond with Block Sequence Response (Category N / Type M) and Message Count Status Response (Category N / Type S) CBOE to start submitting Options data from the latest Sequence Numbers -- Subscribers to accept Quotes and trades disseminated on the output lines. -- Subscribers can request Retransmission for any gaps via the retransmission lines on the DR site. OPRA OPRA GTH to simulate Network Failure scenarios which may result in - - Failure of a subset of OPRA GTH Input lines for CBOE - Momentary disruption in output multicast data dissemination -- CBOE may see Primary input lines disconnected -- Subscribers may see a momentary outage in data flow on the output lines and can request retransmission for the gap In case of a line disconnection, CBOE to reconnect to OPRA GTH and establish sequence numbers by sending Block Sequence Number Status Inquiry Request (Category N / Type L) and Message Count Status Inquiry Request (Category N / Type R) OPRA GTH to accept input connections and respond with latest sequence number via Block Sequence Response (Category N / Type M) and Message Count Status Response (Category N / Type S) Messages CBOE to start submitting Quote and Trade data Quotes and trades accepted and disseminated on the output lines 7 9:15:00 AM End of test OPRA Global Trading Hours (GTH) Server Failure/Data Center Failover Test Plan Test Date & Time: July 25, 2026, 7:30 AM to 9:15 AM ET (Approximate) Hourly Test Script 2 7:45:00 PM - 7:30:00 AM Participant Connectivity/Input CBOE 4 7:45:00 - 7:55:00 AM Single Participant Line Disconnect 5 7:55:00 - 8:15:00 AM Site failover from Primary Data Center to Disaster Recovery Data Center 6 8:30:00 - title: MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: Honeywell International Inc. (HON)
raw_text:
MIAX Emerald — Listing Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: Honeywell International Inc. (HON)
Listing Alert MIAX Emerald June 26, 2026 | 04:01 PM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: Honeywell International Inc. (HON) Honeywell International Inc. (HON) has announced a distribution and a reverse stock split effective for Monday, June 29, 2026. HON2 will be available for trading on the MIAX Options Exchange, MIAX Pearl Options Exchange and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange on Monday, June 29, 2026. HON2 will not be available for trading on the MIAX Emerald Options Exchange. No options on the underlying HON will be available for trading on Monday, June 29, 2026. All GTC orders resting on the MIAX order books in HON will be canceled at the close of business on Friday, June 26, 2026. HON will resume trading on the MIAX Options Exchange, MIAX Pearl Options Exchange, MIAX Emerald Options Exchange and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange on Tuesday, June 30, 2026. Please Note: HON2 options will trade on Cloud 8 on the MIAX Options Exchange, on Cloud 4 on the MIAX Pearl Options Exchange and on Cloud 3 on the MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange. Market Makers can use the Member Firm Portal (MFP) to manage their option class assignments. HON should be used for class assignments in HON2 via MFP All LMM and RMM Option Class Assignments must be entered prior to 6:00 PM ET on the business day immediately preceding the effective date. All changes made after 6:00 PM ET on a given day will be effective two trading days later. MIAX Options and MIAX Emerald Primary Lead Market Maker (PLMM) assignments and un-assignments will not be supported via the MFP. Additional details about the above Honeywell International Inc. (HON) distribution and reverse split are provided in the attached OCC Memo. Please contact MIAX Listings with any questions at Listings@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7308. HON Reverse Split 59250

title: NYSE Group - Trading Halt Rules Updates
raw_text:
NYSE Group - Trading Halt Rules Updates
Markets: NYSE, NYSE AMERICAN EQUITIES, NYSE ARCA EQUITIES, NYSE NATIONAL, NYSE TEXAS; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
NYSE Group equities will implement changes to its Trading Halt Rules as outlined below. Trading Halt Rules Update Beginning August 10, 2026, NYSE Group will adopt updated Trading Halt rules that provide for the following changes: Reorganize existing halt authority and resumption procedures Provide the exchanges with discretion to halt trading in the event of a SIP Outage, Material SIP Latency, or Extraordinary Market Activity Specify rules for Operational Halts that apply to only one market The updated rules may be found in the exchanges' rulebooks beneath the current versions of Rules 7.18 / 7.18E / 7.18-E. Production Implementation Schedule Market Date NYSE, NYSE American - Industry Test* Saturday, August 8, 2026 NYSE American Monday, August 10, 2026 NYSE Arca, NYSE National Tuesday, August 11, 2026 NYSE, NYSE Texas Thursday, August 13, 2026 * A test script for the industry test will be distributed prior to the test. Corporate Action Regulatory Halts In support of 23/5 Trading, NYSE Arca has filed with the SEC rules for mandatory regulatory halts for securities subject to certain corporate actions. These rules ensure that both the Exchange and market participants have adequate time to process complex corporate actions and make the necessary adjustments to systems, orders, quotes and reference data. These rules will become effective upon the launch of 23/5 Trading in December 2026. For further information, refer to the Corporate Actions Halts FAQ . Please contact rmteam@nyse.com with any questions about these updates.

title: NYSE, NYSE Arca - Updated Limit Up Limit Down Tier 1 ETP List July 2026
raw_text:
NYSE, NYSE Arca - Updated Limit Up Limit Down Tier 1 ETP List July 2026
Markets: NYSE, NYSE AMERICAN EQUITIES, NYSE ARCA EQUITIES, NYSE NATIONAL, NYSE TEXAS; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
NYSE Arca and NYSE, in coordination with other listing exchanges, have updated the list of Exchange Traded Products eligible for Tier 1 of the Limit Up Limit Down ("LULD") program, effective July 1, 2026. The updated list of ETPs, along with the additions and deletions from the current Tier 1 list, can be found here . Tier 1 Eligibility Criteria To determine eligibility as a Tier 1 NMS stock, all ETPs across multiple asset classes and issuers were sorted by notional consolidated average daily volume ("CADV"). The list includes domestic equity, international equity, fixed income, currency, and commodities and futures but excludes leveraged ETPs. Notional CADV was calculated by multiplying daily volumes by closing prices each day of the measurement period and then averaging them for the period. The measurement period was the first day of the previous fiscal half year up until one week before the beginning of the next fiscal half year, in this case, January 2, 2026 to June 24, 2026. ETPs, including inverse ETPs, that traded over $2,000,000 CADV were eligible to be included as a Tier 1 NMS stock. Please contact rmteam@nyse.com with any questions about this update.

title: MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: 2x Long VIX Futures ETF (UVIX) (UVIX3)
raw_text:
MIAX Sapphire — Listing Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: 2x Long VIX Futures ETF (UVIX) (UVIX3)
Listing Alert MIAX Sapphire June 30, 2026 | 01:09 PM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Corporate Action Alert: 2x Long VIX Futures ETF (UVIX) (UVIX3) 2x Long VIX Futures ETF (UVIX) has announced a reverse stock split and a distribution effective for Wednesday, July 1, 2026. UVIX1 and UVIX3 will be available for trading on the MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchanges on Wednesday, July 1, 2026. UVIX1 and UVIX3 will not be available for trading on the MIAX Emerald Options Exchange. No options on the underlying UVIX will be available for trading on Wednesday, July 1, 2026. All GTC orders resting on the MIAX order books in UVIX and UVIX3 will be canceled at the close of business on Tuesday, June 30, 2026. UVIX will resume trading on the MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchanges on Thursday, July 2, 2026. Please Note: UVIX1 and UVIX3 options will trade on Cloud 18 on the MIAX Options Exchange, on Cloud 10 on the MIAX Pearl Options Exchange and on Cloud 6 on the MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange. Market Makers can use the Member Firm Portal (MFP) to manage their option class assignments. UVIX should be used for class assignments in UVIX1 and UVIX3 via MFP All LMM and RMM Option Class Assignments must be entered prior to 6:00 PM ET on the business day immediately preceding the effective date. All changes made after 6:00 PM ET on a given day will be effective two trading days later. MIAX Options and MIAX Emerald Primary Lead Market Maker (PLMM) assignments and un-assignments will not be supported via the MFP. Additional details about the above 2x Long VIX Futures ETF (UVIX) reverse stock split and distribution are provided in the attached OCC Memos. Please contact MIAX Listings with any questions at Listings@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7308. UVIX3 Adjustment 59252 UVIX Reverse Split 59251
